commit.message:
fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor: robust ablation + weight analysis + better kernel library

ablation.py:
- ablation_n_kernels: K-fold stratified CV (3 rounds × 5 folds = 15 evals/point)
  instead of 5 random holdout splits → much lower variance
- Consensus kernel ranking averaged over all folds (not 1 seed)
- CI95 computed per n_k for proper confidence band visualization
- weight_analysis(): per-kernel diagnostic — alignment, concentration, nonzero rate
- Extracted _evaluate_svm() and _combine_kernels() helpers

feature_maps.py:
- get_feature_map_library() now returns OrderedDict (was list of tuples)
- Removed extreme alpha values (14, 20) that always cause concentration
- Added pauli_YZX variant, rebalanced alphas to useful range [0.5, 4.0]

notebook 05:
- Uses new ablation_n_kernels with K-fold CV
- Confidence band (fill_between) instead of error bars
- New weight analysis cell with scatter plots (alignment vs weight, concentration vs weight)
- Fixed dict iteration for get_feature_map_library

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: robust solver cascade and missing internal functions

alignment.py:
- Add concentration detection: returns uniform weights when kernels are
  near-constant (exponential concentration), instead of crashing the solver
- Multi-solver cascade: CLARABEL → ECOS → SCS → closed-form fallback
- Use cp.psd_wrap() to avoid CVXPY recomputing PSD decomposition
- Adaptive Tikhonov regularization scaled by matrix Frobenius norm
- Catch all solver exceptions (not just ImportError)

statistical_analysis.py:
- Add _frobenius_alignment() — was imported by ablation.py but missing

visualization.py:
- Add _kernel_off_diagonal_stats() — was imported by ablation.py but missing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

RHIDP-12205: CQA 2.1 compliance for Audit logs in Red Hat Developer Hub (#1877)

* RHIDP-12205: CQA 2.1 compliance for Audit logs in Red Hat Developer Hub title

Apply CQA 2.1 standards for DITA 1.3 compatibility and AEM migration readiness:
- Add short descriptions with [role="_abstract"] to assembly and all modules
- Remove DITA-incompatible block title delimiters (--) from example blocks
- Convert callout list to description list format
- Apply one sentence per line formatting to substep descriptions
- Fix Red Hat style: change "using" to "by using"

Validation results: Vale DITA (0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 suggestions) and Vale Red Hat style (0 errors, 0 warnings)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Fabrice Flore-Thébault <ffloreth@redhat.com>

* Apply suggestions from code review

* RHIDP-12205: Apply code review suggestions and align IDs with titles

Apply all inline code review suggestions from lhite8041 and update module IDs
to follow CQA guidelines (no proc- prefix, match title exactly).

Changes to proc-audit-log-view.adoc:
- Updated ID: proc-audit-log-view → view-audit-logs-in-product-short
- Improved abstract: Split into two sentences for clarity
- Fixed Red Hat style: "using" → "by using" after noun

Changes to proc-forward-audit-log-splunk.adoc:
- Updated title: "Forwarding" → "Forward" (gerund to imperative)
- Updated ID: proc-forward-audit-log-splunk → forward-product-audit-logs-to-splunk
- Combined abstract: Merged abstract and following sentence into single, complete sentence
- Improved AtLeastOnce description: More concise wording per review suggestion

All changes maintain Vale DITA compliance (0 errors, 0 warnings) and address
reviewer feedback for improved clarity and consistency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* RHIDP-12205: Convert audit log configuration from concept to procedure module

Convert con-audit-log-config.adoc from a concept module to a procedure module
following modular documentation templates.

Changes:
- Changed content type: CONCEPT → PROCEDURE
- Updated title: "Configuring audit logs" → "Configure audit logs" (gerund to imperative)
- Updated file name: con-audit-log-config.adoc → proc-configure-audit-logs.adoc
- Updated ID to follow CQA guidelines:
  - Old: con-audit-log-config_{context}
  - New: configure-audit-logs-for-product-short-on-ocp-short_{context}
- Restructured content to follow procedure template:
  - Added Prerequisites section
  - Converted description lists to numbered procedure steps with substeps
  - Moved Additional resources links into procedure steps for context
- Updated include statement in assembly-audit-log.adoc

The original content described configuration steps, which is procedural content,
so this conversion aligns the module type with its actual content.

Vale DITA validation: 0 errors, 0 warnings
Vale Red Hat style: 0 errors (4 URL warnings are false positives)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Fabrice Flore-Thébault <ffloreth@redhat.com>

* RHIDP-12205: Align procedure module file names with titles in audit log

Renamed procedure modules to match their titles following CQA 2.1 naming conventions:
- proc-audit-log-view.adoc → proc-view-audit-logs-in-product-short.adoc
- proc-forward-audit-log-splunk.adoc → proc-forward-product-audit-logs-to-splunk.adoc

Updated include statements in assembly-audit-log.adoc to reference renamed files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* RHIDP-12205: Simplify abstract and use terminal source type for commands

Simplified the abstract in proc-forward-product-audit-logs-to-splunk.adoc from 217 to 113 characters by removing implementation details.

Changed all terminal commands from [source,bash] to [source,terminal] to follow the correct convention for terminal commands versus bash scripts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* RHIDP-12205: Remove passive voice from procedure

Converted passive voice to active voice in proc-forward-product-audit-logs-to-splunk.adoc to improve clarity and follow Red Hat style guidelines:
- "where the captured logs are sent" → "where to send the captured logs"
- "Tuning can be applied" → "You can apply tuning"
- "are re-sent" → "the forwarder re-sends"
- "logs are duplicated" → "forwarder might duplicate some logs"
- "logs are being forwarded" → "Splunk instance receives logs"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* RHIDP-12205: Update CQA.md with procedure writing guidelines

Added new sections to CQA.md based on learnings from audit log work:
- Source code block types: Use [source,terminal] for terminal commands, not [source,bash]
- Voice and tense in procedures: Avoid passive voice, use present tense
- Abstract guidelines: Keep concise (50-300 chars), focus on essential action

These guidelines help ensure procedures follow Red Hat style and DITA compliance requirements.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Fabrice Flore-Thébault <ffloreth@redhat.com>

repository.name: red-hat-developers-documentation-rhdh
repository.description: Red Hat Developer Hub documentation - upstream sources for https://gitlab.cee.redhat.com/red-hat-developers-documentation/rhdh

commit.message:
feat: V6 — action masking only, revert hyperparams to V4

V5 regressed to 10% success (V4 was 30%) due to changing too many
hyperparams at once. V6 keeps action masking (25D→13D) but reverts
gamma=0.8, gae_lambda=0.9, ent_coef=0.0, vf_coef=0.5. Trains for
100M steps to isolate action masking benefit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use relative API URLs via Next.js proxy and improve kline/sandbox

- frontend: replace hardcoded localhost:8000 with "" (relative URL) across
  all pages/components, routing API calls through Next.js rewrites proxy
- frontend/next.config.ts: add /api/* → http://localhost:18000/api/* rewrite
- frontend/package.json: dev server binds to 0.0.0.0 for external access
- frontend: increase default klines limit from 200 to 1000
- backend/market_data.py: only do incremental fetch when cache is full;
  fetch from scratch when cache is insufficient to avoid stale data
- backend/sandbox.py: allow safe dunder attrs (__init__, __class__, etc.)
  while still blocking dangerous system-level dunders

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: add disk cache, parallel kernel computation and Aer backend

- kernel_matrix.py: disk cache (.npy files), compute_kernel_matrix_parallel with joblib
- quantum_kernel.py: Aer multi-thread backend (statevector, max_parallel_threads=0)
- scripts/precompute_kernels.py: one-shot script to pre-fill cache before using notebooks
- notebooks/03: use parallel+cached kernel loading, fix get_feature_map_library iteration bug

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(db): align seed SQL columns with current entity schema

Organizations: remove non-existent columns (legal_name, code, features),
add slug + status, fix settings/limits JSONB structure.
Users: remove language/is_active columns, fix ON CONFLICT to use email.
Products: base_price→selling_price, remove is_available, fix category
enum values (beverages→cold_drinks, coffee→hot_drinks).
Machines: code→machine_number, slot_count→max_product_slots, remove
is_active/payment_methods, add accepts_cash/card/qr booleans,
fix type enum (beverage→drink).

Fix testimonials bubble: use clip-path instead of overflow:hidden on wrapper

overflow:hidden on .tmt-avatar-wrap was clipping the speech bubble.

circular but wrapper no longer clips its children, so bubble appears.

commit.message:
Fix TMI PER en sortie capital + frais souscription SCPI sur réinvestissement

1. PER sortie capital : la TMI retraite incluait une rente PER fictive même
   en mode croquerCapital, gonflant la TMI (ex: 30% au lieu de 11%).
   Fix : rentePerBruteEstimee = 0 quand croquerCapital = true.

2. SCPI : les distributions réinvesties composaient à 4.5% sans friction.
   En réalité, réinvestir en SCPI coûte ~10% de frais de souscription.
   Fix : rendementScpiEffectif = rendementScpi × (1 − partDistrib × frais),
   soit ~4.1% au lieu de 4.5% (~0.4 pt/an de surestimation corrigée).

3. Documenté la limitation connue du drawdown par annuité fixe vs simulation
   avec rendements distincts par enveloppe (nécessiterait une bisection).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ingest): retry reset when desktop still linked after startup

Initial reset call fails silently when Signal Desktop container isn't
reachable yet. Desktop then comes up still linked from previous session,
and the QR poll loop waits 120s for an unlink that never happens.

feat: upgrade action node with Ruby sandbox and context updates

Action node now accepts Ruby code evaluated in a sandboxed environment
with access to input, context, output, and a dig() helper. Supports

10-second timeout for safety. Update docs with expression system and
action node usage.

fix(telegram): fallback to plain text when Markdown parsing fails

Telegram's Markdown parser chokes on underscores in technical text
(e.g. reply_to_message, message_id). Now retries without parse_mode
when the API returns "can't parse entities".

feat: Custom React email builder replacing GrapesJS

Full drag-and-drop email builder built from scratch with React:
- 15 block types (text, image, button, columns, hero, etc.)
- @dnd-kit/sortable for drag reorder + HTML5 drag from sidebar
- Inline text editing, property panels per block type
- Table-based email HTML export with inline styles

- Content/Styles sidebar tabs like Klaviyo

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix selection visibility - replace List with ScrollView+LazyVStack

SwiftUI List(selection:) was overriding custom row backgrounds with
its own highlight, hiding multi-selection visuals. Replaced with
ScrollView + LazyVStack which gives full control over row rendering.
Cmd+click, Shift+click, Alt+click selection now works visually.

Fix click selection modifiers: Cmd=add, Shift=range, Alt=remove

- Cmd+click: toggle item in selection (add or remove)
- Shift+click: select all items between cursor and clicked item
- Alt/Option+click: remove item from selection
- Plain click: clear selection and move cursor

feat: add expression resolver and n8n-like expression editor UI

Add ExpressionResolver service to resolve {{ input.x }}, {{ context.x }},
and {{ nodes.<id>.x }} expressions at runtime in node configs. Integrate
into NodeRunner with resolved_config pattern.

Add ExpressionEditor React component with variable picker, expression
highlighting, and auto-open on {{ typing. Add EntryConditionsEditor
component for trigger entry conditions management in the toolbar.

Fix expression picker variable insertion in Shadow DOM by using
getRootNode() for outside-click detection and preventing stale closures.

Add SSH tunnel support for PostgreSQL connection (#38)

The dbsync postgres service is being decommissioned. The replacement
instance requires SSH tunnel access. This adds in-process SSH tunneling
via the sshtunnel library, activated by setting SSH_HOST env var.
